name: pii-scrub-audit
question: Does every emitted log line (from tracing subscribers) pass through the canonical 20-pattern PII scrubber installed by feedbackmonk-tracing::install_global_subscriber? Has the pattern set drifted from the canonical source ported byte-for-byte from GitCellar?

output.schema: PASS or FAIL <count> with file:line / hash mismatch detail on FAIL; exit code 0 on PASS, non-zero on FAIL

consumer_scope: P1+ all backend crates (every Stage 1+ worker emitting logs; CI gate)
fallback: Probe A: grep every .rs file under crates/ for the pattern (tracing_subscriber::fmt\(|tracing_subscriber::registry\(|impl ... Layer<...> for ...) OUTSIDE crates/feedbackmonk-tracing/. Probe B: read crates/feedbackmonk-tracing/src/scrubber.rs, extract each CANONICAL_PATTERNS tuple (name, regex, replacement), serialise as name\tregex\treplacement\n, SHA-256 the bytes, compare to .claude/oracles/pii-scrub-audit/expected_hash.txt.

provenance.rationale: FR-FBR-10 + DEC-FBR-01 Persona D (privacy). Three-leg defense per D-FBR-02: (1) type-system chokepoint in feedbackmonk_tracing::install_global_subscriber; (2) this oracle (AST probe + hash probe); (3) clippy + cargo-deny rejecting direct tracing_subscriber::fmt() builder calls outside the binary's main entrypoint.
